title: Logfia heterantha
taxonomy: Logfia heterantha is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Logfia heterantha among the flowering plants, specifically in the order Asterales. Logfia heterantha belongs to the subfamily Gnaphaloideae, which is a division of the Asteraceae family. The genus Logfia is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae within the subfamily Gnaphaloideae. The exact classification may still be subject to revision as plant taxonomy continues to evolve.

intro: Logfia heterantha, also known as heteranthera logfia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Logfia heterantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Logfia heterantha is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and colds.
